description: Close the productization gap from the current TravelAgent MVP to an internal beta by adding real travel constraints, trust signals, execution checklists, version history, and quality operations.

userStories.title: Extend task memory with real trip constraint fields
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want task memory to persist origin, start date, end date, and traveler type so that planning uses real travel constraints instead of only free-form text.
acceptanceCriteria: Task memory or equivalent persistence includes origin, startDate, endDate, and travelers fields
Existing conversation detail reads remain backward compatible when those fields are absent
Chat and conversation detail payloads expose the new fields consistently
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add structured brief fields to the chat request flow
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want the chat flow to accept structured trip brief updates so that the UI can send constrained edits without relying on prompt wording.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat request handling accepts structured brief fields or an equivalent patch object for destination, dates, budget, travelers, and preferences
Structured brief updates merge into persisted task memory within the same conversation
The planner still works for pure free-form text requests
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add trip brief editor to the chat workspace
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit destination, dates, budget, and traveler info directly in the UI so that I can correct the trip brief without crafting another complex message.
acceptanceCriteria: The chat workspace shows editable fields for destination, dates, budget, travelers, and preferences
Missing brief fields are visually marked instead of rendering as silent blanks
Saving or applying the brief triggers a chat request with the structured values
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-04-17 in a headless browser session against the local-demo profile by applying brief fields from the workspace and confirming the structured request payload.

userStories.title: Generate dated itinerary windows when dates are known
userStories.description: As a user, I want daily plans tied to real dates so that the itinerary feels like an actual trip instead of an abstract day sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: When startDate is present, generated itinerary days include real dates in order
When dates are missing, the planner remains usable and marks the result as estimate mode
Task memory and plan payloads keep date-derived fields aligned
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Display real travel dates in the itinerary UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see actual dates next to Day 1 and Day 2 so that I can judge the itinerary against a real departure window.
acceptanceCriteria: The itinerary panel shows both day index and actual date when a dated plan exists
Estimate-mode plans clearly explain that dates are not locked yet
Date rendering stays consistent across overview and daily itinerary sections
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-04-17 in a headless browser session against the local-demo profile by confirming itinerary cards render actual dates for Day 1 and Day 2.

userStories.title: Standardize trust metadata on travel recommendations
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want hotels, POIs, and routes to expose consistent source and confidence fields so that the UI can explain how trustworthy each recommendation is.
acceptanceCriteria: Hotels, POI matches, and route objects include stable trust metadata such as source, confidence, freshness, or equivalent fields
Fallback or estimated recommendations are explicitly marked in structured payloads
New trust fields are backward compatible for existing consumers
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Surface recommendation trust states in the UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want visible trust states on hotels, POIs, and routes so that I can tell which recommendations are grounded and which still need confirmation.
acceptanceCriteria: Hotel cards, POI detail areas, and route detail areas display a consistent trust state label or badge
Fallback recommendations include explanatory copy instead of only a raw source code
Trust styling remains consistent across chat, itinerary, and map-related surfaces
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-04-17 in a headless browser session against the local-demo profile by confirming trust-state copy is rendered on stay recommendations.

userStories.title: Add partial replan request support
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want the API to accept a scoped replan request so that users can regenerate only the affected part of a trip.
acceptanceCriteria: The chat request flow accepts a partial replan scope for at least hotel area and a specific day
Scoped replan requests identify the target day or planning slice explicitly
Unscoped requests continue to behave as full replans
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement partial replan for hotel area and day-level changes
userStories.description: As a user, I want the system to regenerate only the hotel area or one day plan so that a local correction does not discard the rest of the itinerary.
acceptanceCriteria: Scoped hotel-area replans preserve unaffected daily itinerary structure
Scoped day replans preserve unaffected days and stable version context
The response indicates which part of the plan was regenerated
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add partial replan controls to the UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to trigger a hotel-area or day-specific replan from the workspace so that local corrections are discoverable and low effort.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI provides scoped replan actions for at least hotel area and individual days
After a scoped replan, the UI clearly marks which section was refreshed
Unchanged itinerary sections remain visible rather than resetting the full workspace
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-04-17 in a headless browser session against the local-demo profile by confirming hotel-area and day-level replan controls both render and surface refreshed-section banners.

userStories.title: Generate a pre-departure checklist with each structured plan
userStories.description: As a user, I want the system to generate a pre-departure checklist so that I can move from reading a plan to preparing an actual trip.
acceptanceCriteria: Structured planner responses include a checklist or equivalent block for accommodation, transport, tickets, weather, and travel essentials
Checklist items distinguish confirmed and pending states
Checklist generation is available on every structured planner result, not only on manual export
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist checklist state for a conversation
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want checklist state stored with the conversation so that users do not lose pre-departure progress after reload.
acceptanceCriteria: Checklist item state persists for the active conversation
Conversation detail reads return the latest checklist state
Older conversations without checklist state remain readable
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render an interactive pre-departure checklist panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and toggle a trip checklist in the UI so that the workspace helps me prepare, not only generate.
acceptanceCriteria: The workspace shows a checklist panel or equivalent section for the active plan
Users can toggle checklist item state and see pending counts update immediately
The UI clearly distinguishes confirmed and pending items
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-04-17 in a headless browser session against the local-demo profile by toggling checklist state and observing pending counts update immediately.

userStories.title: Persist plan version snapshots
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want every important plan regeneration to create a version snapshot so that users and feedback analytics can refer to a stable plan revision.
acceptanceCriteria: Generating a new plan or scoped replan saves a version snapshot with timestamp and input summary
Feedback records can reference a stable plan version identifier
Existing single-plan conversation reads remain backward compatible
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expose recent plan version diffs
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want an API view of recent plan changes so that the UI can show what changed between the last two versions.
acceptanceCriteria: Conversation detail or a companion endpoint exposes the latest plan version and its previous version diff
The diff summarizes at least date, hotel, budget, and key stop changes
The diff response remains usable when only one version exists
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render recent version comparison in the UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to compare the latest plan with the previous version so that I understand what the system actually changed.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI shows a readable recent-version comparison for dates, hotel area, budget, and key stop changes
The comparison does not require raw JSON diff output
The comparison is available after a regeneration without leaving the workspace
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-04-17 in a headless browser session against the local-demo profile by confirming the recent version comparison panel renders dates, hotel area, and budget summaries after scoped replans.

userStories.title: Add internal beta quality scenario fixtures and runner
userStories.description: As a product team member, I want standard internal-beta travel scenarios to be replayable so that we can detect regressions in core cities and common use cases.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a reusable scenario set covering city short trips, family travel, low-budget planning, and screenshot-assisted intake
A standard command runs the scenarios and records whether they returned a structured plan, fallback, or high-risk outcome
Scenario outputs are readable by the product team without manual log digging
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-04-16 by running the scenario replay against the new local-demo startup profile.

userStories.title: Add backend filters for quality operations summary
userStories.description: As an operator, I want feedback and quality summaries filtered by city, agent, version, and reason labels so that I can quickly isolate the most harmful internal-beta failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Feedback summary or export APIs support filters for destination, agent type, version range, and reason labels
Summary output includes frequent failure buckets and representative cases
Structured export remains available for offline analysis
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add quality operations filters to the feedback UI
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a filtered feedback operations view in the product so that internal-beta issue review does not require ad hoc SQL or raw exports.
acceptanceCriteria: The feedback operations UI supports filters for destination, agent type, version scope, and reason labels
The view shows top failure reasons and representative findings using the filtered dataset
The interface remains usable without exposing raw developer logs
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified on 2026-04-17 in a headless browser session against the local-demo profile by submitting feedback and filtering the operations panel by destination, agent, scope, and reason label.
